attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a clean fork in a drawer.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

Objects and Their Types:
- Agent: A robotic agent is present in the scene, initially not located at any object.
- Receptacles: The scene contains a sink (stainless steel, double basin, with a faucet) and a set of wooden cabinets below the countertop, which are likely to function as drawers or storage units (receptacles). The sink is explicitly defined as a receptacle in the domain.
- Objects: A fork (metallic, silver-colored) is visible on the countertop to the right of a loaf of bread. A knife (metallic, with a blue handle) is located on the countertop near the sink. A loaf of bread (brown, rectangular) and a head of lettuce (green) are on the countertop. A round plate with a brown substance (possibly sauce or soup) is also on the countertop. An egg is in the left basin of the sink. A soap dispenser (purple with a brown pump) is on the countertop near the sink.

Quantities of Each Object Type:
- Receptacles: 2 (sink and wooden cabinets/drawers)
- Objects: 1 fork, 1 knife, 1 loaf of bread, 1 head of lettuce, 1 plate with brown substance, 1 egg, 1 soap dispenser

Spatial Relationships:
- The fork is on the countertop, to the right of the loaf of bread.
- The knife is on the countertop, to the right of the sink.
- The egg is in the left basin of the sink.
- The soap dispenser is on the countertop, to the right of the sink.
- The wooden cabinets are below the countertop, likely functioning as drawers.
- The robotic agent is not initially at any object’s location.

Current State of Each Object Relevant to the Planning Task:
- The fork is not clean (not in the sink and not marked as clean).
- The fork is not held by the agent.
- The fork is not in any receptacle.
- The sink is not currently being used for cleaning.
- The wooden cabinets (drawers) are closed (not opened).

Colors, Positions, and Other Visual Properties:
- The fork is metallic and silver-colored, positioned on the countertop to the right of the bread.
- The knife has a blue handle and is metallic, located on the countertop near the sink.
- The sink is stainless steel with a double basin and a faucet.
- The wooden cabinets below the countertop are brown and have visible handles, indicating they can be opened.
- The countertop is a light gray color, providing a neutral background for the objects.

The task requires the robot to clean the fork in the sink and then place it in a drawer. The fork is currently on the countertop and needs to be picked up, cleaned in the sink, and then placed in one of the wooden cabinets (drawers). The sink is available for cleaning, and the wooden cabinets are closed and need to be opened to place the fork inside.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem clean_fork_and_put_in_drawer)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        sink - sink
        drawer - receptacle
        fork - object
        knife - knife
        bread - object
        lettuce - object
        plate - object
        egg - object
        soap_dispenser - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot drawer))
        (not (atLocation robot fork))
        (not (atLocation robot knife))
        (not (atLocation robot bread))
        (not (atLocation robot lettuce))
        (not (atLocation robot plate))
        (not (atLocation robot egg))
        (not (atLocation robot soap_dispenser))

        ; Fork is on countertop (not in any receptacle, not held)
        (not (inReceptacle fork sink))
        (not (inReceptacle fork drawer))
        (not (holds robot fork))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (isClean fork))

        ; Sink is not opened (if it were openable, but sink is not specified as openable in domain, so skip)
        ; Drawer is closed (assuming it's openable as per typical drawer behavior)
        (openable drawer)
        (not (opened drawer))

        ; Egg is in sink (as per scene)
        (inReceptacle egg sink)

        ; Knife, bread, lettuce, plate, soap dispenser are on countertop (not held, not in receptacle)
        (not (holds robot knife))
        (not (inReceptacle knife sink))
        (not (inReceptacle knife drawer))
        (not (holds robot bread))
        (not (inReceptacle bread sink))
        (not (inReceptacle bread drawer))
        (not (holds robot lettuce))
        (not (inReceptacle lettuce sink))
        (not (inReceptacle lettuce drawer))
        (not (holds robot plate))
        (not (inReceptacle plate sink))
        (not (inReceptacle plate drawer))
        (not (holds robot soap_dispenser))
        (not (inReceptacle soap_dispenser sink))
        (not (inReceptacle soap_dispenser drawer))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle fork drawer)
        (isClean fork)
    ))
)
```

attempts.plan:
(gotolocation robot fork)
(pickupobject robot fork)
(gotolocation robot sink)
(cleanobject robot sink fork)
(gotolocation robot drawer)
(putobjectinreceptacle robot fork drawer)
